text.title: Indie Portfolio Scorecard Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Indie Portfolio Scorecard application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every product row MUST populate all five columns: MRR, growth %, support hours, founder hours, joy. Missing columns invalidate the row.
rule.rationale: Scorecard-discipline research — anchors Indie Portfolio Scorecard rule `five-columns-required`.

rule.statement: Growth % MUST be computed over a 90-day window. Shorter windows are noise; longer windows lag too much.
rule.rationale: Time-series research — anchors Indie Portfolio Scorecard rule `ninety-day-growth-window`.

rule.statement: Founder hours/week MUST be honestly self-reported; 'less than I think' bias inflates ROI estimates. Calibrate against 1 week of time tracking before the first run.
rule.rationale: Self-reporting calibration — anchors Indie Portfolio Scorecard rule `hours-self-report-honest`.

rule.statement: The scorecard MUST be reviewed monthly. Quarterly = signal lags too long; weekly = noise.
rule.rationale: Cadence-research practice — anchors Indie Portfolio Scorecard rule `monthly-review-cadence`.

rule.statement: Each scorecard review MUST emit one decision per product: grow / maintain / kill. No-decision rows are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Decision-record discipline — anchors Indie Portfolio Scorecard rule `explicit-decision-per-product`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
